task_completion.description: Agent creates a script to programmatically interact with the VMS API.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal showing a script (e.g., Python, bash) being written or executed to handle the API requests.

task_completion.description: Agent authenticates to obtain a bearer token.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or terminal commands showing a POST request to 'https://localhost:7001/rest/v1/login/sessions' with username 'admin' and password 'Admin1234!', and logic to extract the token.

task_completion.description: Agent queries all required API endpoints with SSL verification disabled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code or terminal commands showing GET requests to '/rest/v1/system/info', '/rest/v1/servers', '/rest/v1/devices', '/rest/v1/users', '/rest/v1/storages', and '/rest/v1/system/settings' using the bearer token and disabling SSL verification (e.g., verify=False in Python or -k in curl).

task_completion.description: Agent compiles the data into the required JSON structure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Code building a dictionary/JSON object with the exact top-level keys: 'report_metadata', 'server_info', 'cameras', 'users', 'storage', 'system_settings', including the required summaries (e.g., total_count, online_count).

task_completion.description: Agent saves the formatted JSON report to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal command (e.g., 'cat ~/reports/system_health_report.json') or file editor showing the final report saved at '~/reports/system_health_report.json' with 2-space indentation.

integrity.description: Agent used a script or CLI commands to generate the report programmatically.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a script or a sequence of curl/jq commands that generate the final output, rather than the agent manually typing the JSON content.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual local API.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script targets 'https://localhost:7001' and handles the self-signed SSL certificate appropriately, rather than mocking the data.

integrity.description: Agent dynamically extracted data from the API responses.
integrity.visual_evidence: The script parses the API responses to populate the JSON fields (e.g., extracting systemName, counting cameras/users) instead of hardcoding values like 'GymAnythingVMS' or specific counts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Nx Witness VMS is running at https://localhost:7001. The agent must authenticate using POST /rest/v1/login/sessions with username 'admin' and password 'Admin1234!'. The required endpoints to query are /rest/v1/system/info, /rest/v1/servers, /rest/v1/devices, /rest/v1/users, /rest/v1/storages, and /rest/v1/system/settings. SSL verification must be disabled (e.g., using verify=False in Python or -k in curl). The final report must be saved to ~/reports/system_health_report.json with the exact keys: report_metadata, server_info, cameras, users, storage, system_settings. The system name is 'GymAnythingVMS', there are 3 cameras and 6 users (admin + 5 created).
